title: Quiz: Optimal Iterations
description: Test your understanding of optimal iteration count in Grover's algorithm

questions.question: For N=1024 items with one solution, the optimal number of iterations is approximately:
The initial angle theta between |s> and |s'> satisfies:
questions.explanation: The amplitude of |w> in |s> is 1/sqrt(N), so sin(theta) = 1/sqrt(N).

questions.question: Each Grover iteration rotates the state by:
questions.explanation: Each application of G = D*O rotates the state by 2*theta toward |w>.

questions.question: Running exactly 2x the optimal iterations will give approximately zero probability of finding the solution.
questions.explanation: At 2*r_opt, the angle is approximately pi (180 deg), giving sin^2(pi) = 0. The state has rotated past |w> and is now pointing away from it.

questions.question: What happens if you run more iterations than optimal?
questions.explanation: Over-rotation causes the state to rotate past |w>, decreasing the amplitude of the solution state.

questions.question: For N = 256, the optimal iteration angle ((2r+1)*theta at r_opt) is closest to:
questions.explanation: Optimal iterations are chosen so (2r+1)*theta = pi/2 = 90 degrees, maximizing sin^2.

questions.question: The oscillation period (iterations to return to initial probability) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Period T = pi/theta and theta is approximately 1/sqrt(N), so T is approximately pi*sqrt(N).

questions.question: After r iterations, the success probability is given by:
questions.explanation: The state after r iterations has amplitude sin((2r+1)*theta) for |w>, so probability is sin^2((2r+1)*theta).
